repo_prior_art.cite: Q1_StaggeredMinimalBasis: staggered switch-on coefficient decompositions under exact no-anticipation-style history conventions, not bounded anticipation support intervals

published_prior_art.cite: AugustinGutknechtLiu2025: anticipation/misclassification bias but no covariate-conditional sharp box-support decomposition

repo_prior_art.cite: q1_partial_did_unobserved_trends: aggregate pretrend-summary sufficiency/nonsharpness, not bounded anticipation boxes with comparison-cohort contamination

published_prior_art.cite: RambachanRoth2023: general DID partial-ID sensitivity for trend violations, not this staggered anticipation-bias operator with shared-coordinate aggregation

repo_prior_art.cite: Q1_StaggeredMinimalBasis: no-anticipation staggered switch-on algebra, but not zero-bound recovery from a bounded-anticipation interval

published_prior_art.cite: CallawaySantAnna2021 and DCDH2020: standard point-ID/no-anticipation benchmarks recovered as a boundary case

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The decomposition omits an explicit normalization assumption on comparison weights; without sum_h omega_{h|g,t,b}(x)=1, a residual untreated-trend term remains after applying baseline parallel trends.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add a formal comparison-weight assumption: for each x, weights over R_{g,t,b} are defined on positive-overlap comparison cells and sum to one, allowing signed weights only where the recovery theorem explicitly needs them.
State a closed-form aggregate support formula with pooled signed coefficients, not only prose, so Conjecture 1 has an auditable finite-dimensional target.
Add a small three-cohort worked corollary where a not-yet-treated comparison cohort is anticipatory at t but not b, showing the control-cohort term changes interval width.

recommended_next_step: Patch the comparison-weight normalization and make the aggregate support-function formula explicit, then rerun novelty review.
